meaning: n.定量,配给量,定量配给v.配给,分发,实行定量配给
examples: The meat ration was down to one pound per person per week.
I've had my ration of problems for one day—you deal with it!
The new cities and towns, squeezed by their walls, had to know and order time in order to organize collective activity and ration space.
The most recent such shortage, in 2009, required public health officials to ration rabies shots.
Susan managed to procure ration coupons for icing sugar and real eggs for the meringue.
